buginformation.summary: "Visible" checkbox label on WindowEditor not externalized
buginformation.description: "Visible" checkbox label on WindowEditor not externalized for translation.

buginformation.summary: Checkbox labels on MenuContributionEditor not externalized
buginformation.description: "To Be Rendered" and "Visible" checkbox labels on MenuContributionEditor not externalized for translation.

buginformation.summary: SideValue labels in TrimBarEditor not externalized
buginformation.description:
SideValue labels "Top", "Bottom", "Left", "Right" in TrimBarEditor not externalized for translation.
Labels hard-coded in: eclipse.platform.ui/bundles/org.eclipse.e4.ui.model.workbench/src/org/eclipse/e4/ui/model/application/ui/SideValue.java

buginformation.summary: ItemType labels in ToolItemEditor not externalized
buginformation.description:
ItemType labels "Push", "Check", "Radio" in ToolItemEditor not externalized for translation.
Labels hard-coded in: eclipse.platform.ui/bundles/org.eclipse.e4.ui.model.workbench/src/org/eclipse/e4/ui/model/application/ui/menu/ItemType.java

buginformation.summary: e4 TrimBarRenderer causes EMF ConcurrentModificationException on platform startup
buginformation.description:
This stack trace comes up in our Eclipse 4.20-based product on (clean) startup.
java.util.ConcurrentModificationException at org.eclipse.emf.common.util.AbstractEList$EIterator.checkModCount(AbstractEList.java:751) at org.eclipse.emf.common.util.AbstractEList$EIterator.doNext(AbstractEList.java:699) at org.eclipse.emf.common.util.AbstractEList$EIterator.next(AbstractEList.java:685) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.workbench.renderers.swt.SWTPartRenderer.processContents(SWTPartRenderer.java:70) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.workbench.renderers.swt.TrimBarRenderer.processContents(TrimBarRenderer.java:109) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.internal.workbench.swt.PartRenderingEngine.safeCreateGui(PartRenderingEngine.java:673) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.internal.workbench.swt.PartRenderingEngine$1.run(PartRenderingEngine.java:544) at org.eclipse.core.runtime.SafeRunner.run(SafeRunner.java:45) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.internal.workbench.swt.PartRenderingEngine.createGui(PartRenderingEngine.java:528) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.workbench.renderers.swt.WBWRenderer.processContents(WBWRenderer.java:688) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.internal.workbench.swt.PartRenderingEngine.safeCreateGui(PartRenderingEngine.java:673) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.internal.workbench.swt.PartRenderingEngine.safeCreateGui(PartRenderingEngine.java:765) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.internal.workbench.swt.PartRenderingEngine$2.run(PartRenderingEngine.java:730) at org.eclipse.core.runtime.SafeRunner.run(SafeRunner.java:45) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.internal.workbench.swt.PartRenderingEngine.createGui(PartRenderingEngine.java:714) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.internal.workbench.swt.PartRenderingEngine$5.run(PartRenderingEngine.java:1083) at org.eclipse.core.databinding.observable.Realm.runWithDefault(Realm.java:338) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.internal.workbench.swt.PartRenderingEngine.run(PartRenderingEngine.java:1046) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.internal.workbench.E4Workbench.createAndRunUI(E4Workbench.java:155) at org.eclipse.ui.internal.Workbench.lambda$3(Workbench.java:644) at org.eclipse.core.databinding.observable.Realm.runWithDefault(Realm.java:338) at org.eclipse.ui.internal.Workbench.createAndRunWorkbench(Workbench.java:551) at org.eclipse.ui.PlatformUI.createAndRunWorkbench(PlatformUI.java:156) at org.eclipse.ui.internal.ide.application.IDEApplication.start(IDEApplication.java:152) at org.eclipse.equinox.internal.app.EclipseAppHandle.run(EclipseAppHandle.java:203) at org.eclipse.core.runtime.internal.adaptor.EclipseAppLauncher.runApplication(EclipseAppLauncher.java:134) at org.eclipse.core.runtime.internal.adaptor.EclipseAppLauncher.start(EclipseAppLauncher.java:104) at org.eclipse.core.runtime.adaptor.EclipseStarter.run(EclipseStarter.java:401) at org.eclipse.core.runtime.adaptor.EclipseStarter.run(EclipseStarter.java:255) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:62) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:43) at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:566) at org.eclipse.equinox.launcher.Main.invokeFramework(Main.java:654) at org.eclipse.equinox.launcher.Main.basicRun(Main.java:591) at org.eclipse.equinox.launcher.Main.run(Main.java:1462)

buginformation.summary: TrimContributionEditor displays unexternalized element names
buginformation.description: TrimContributionEditor displays unexternalized element names "ToolBar", "ToolControl" instead of using EClassLabelProvider like other editors.

buginformation.summary: VWindowControlEditor displays unexternalized element names
buginformation.description: VWindowControlEditor displays unexternalized element names "PerspectiveStack", "PartSashContainer", "PartStack", "Part", "Area" instead of using EClassLabelProvider like other editors.

buginformation.summary: Add a LRU resource cache for jface
buginformation.description: Eclipse IDE loads the same images (toolbar icons) over and over again because it decreases the reference count to 0 before applying the same images again to the toolbar. The idea is to either keep references to a fixed set of most used icons or simply automatically determine those by a LRU cache. The cache must be limited to avoid too many OS handles. By using SoftReferences it can also be avoided to accidentally cache too many big images which may end up in Out of Memory Error. In a first test a little cache of 300 cached images was enough to never trigger reload during typical actions in jdt ui.

buginformation.summary: Large file alternative editor support doesn't open with selected editor
Remove unused icons from e4 FilteredTree
buginformation.description: AFAICS the icons created in FilteredTree are never accessed. Suggest to delete them and their static initialization block.

buginformation.summary: Change default to use the external browser instead of the internal browser of Eclipse
buginformation.description:
Currently Eclipse preferences states to use the internal browser instead of the external browser.
Is this useful? I never, ever use the internal browser in Eclipse, it the normal browser buttons, preferences, etc.
If more people think the same way, we should change the default to use the default system browser.

buginformation.summary: NPE in LazyResourceManager.shouldBeCached
buginformation.description:
We see few NPE's coming after opening/closing various wizards in our application tests.
All NPE's are looking very similar:
!ENTRY org.eclipse.jface 4 4 2021-11-06 02:36:56.471 !MESSAGE NullPointerException !STACK 0 java.lang.NullPointerException at org.eclipse.jface.resource.LazyResourceManager.shouldBeCached(LazyResourceManager.java:78) at org.eclipse.jface.resource.LazyResourceManager.destroy(LazyResourceManager.java:114) at org.eclipse.jface.resource.ResourceManager.destroyImage(ResourceManager.java:224) at org.eclipse.jface.wizard.Wizard.dispose(Wizard.java:208) at org.eclipse.jface.wizard.WizardDialog.hardClose(WizardDialog.java:878) at org.eclipse.jface.wizard.WizardDialog.finishPressed(WizardDialog.java:840) at org.eclipse.jface.wizard.WizardDialog.buttonPressed(WizardDialog.java:472) at org.eclipse.jface.dialogs.Dialog.lambda$0(Dialog.java:619) at org.eclipse.swt.events.SelectionListener$1.widgetSelected(SelectionListener.java:84) at org.eclipse.swt.widgets.TypedListener.handleEvent(TypedListener.java:252) at org.eclipse.swt.widgets.EventTable.sendEvent(EventTable.java:89) at org.eclipse.swt.widgets.Display.sendEvent(Display.java:5889) at org.eclipse.swt.widgets.Widget.sendEvent(Widget.java:1515) at org.eclipse.swt.widgets.Widget.sendEvent(Widget.java:1541)
The problem is that some wizards were initialized with null descriptor (which is allowed), but on dispose that fails to release image for null descriptor in the new LazyResourceManager.destroy() code.
Note: JFaceResources.getResources().createImageWithDefault(defaultImageDescriptor) which is called in org.eclipse.jface.wizard.Wizard.getDefaultPageImage() allows to create an image for null descriptor.
I have a patch.

buginformation.summary: Rework "Large file alternative editor selection" story to allow explicit editor choice by user
buginformation.description:
If I configure:
org.eclipse.ui.workbench/LARGE_DOC_SIZE_FOR_EDITORS=1048576
and open a large file by double clicking it, I get a dialog to select an alternative editor for this large file. This works as expected.
However, if I right click the file and choose 'Open With' and then select a specific editor from the menu, then I still get the alternative editor selection dialog. This feels weird to me, as I specifically selected the editor I want. This means that there is no way to open a large file with a specific editor without selecting that editor in the dialog.
Could the explicit editor choice then be used rather than that the alternative editor dialog is shown, if I explicitly choose an editor as described? Does that make sense to you?

buginformation.summary: Accessibility Issues With "Restart notification" After Theme Change
buginformation.description:
After a theme change eclipse needs to be re-started. In the past the user was notified about this with a blocking dialog. with bug 564335 a none-blocking notification was introduced instead of the blocking dialog.
What was the exact reason for this?
This new implementation has issue with accessibility. 1.) The none-blocking dialog is not read by screen readers (and this is by intention) 2.) The none-blocking dialog is not reachable via keyboard navigation 3.) The restart information is also shown on the preference page (with a little yellow triangle with a tooltip). Also it seems that this tooltip is not read by screenreaders.
Vision impaired users most probably don't get this information.
There are additional functional issues: A.) If you change the theme multiple times without closing the dialog you get multiple dialogs on top of each other.
To be honest I found the old implementation perfectly fine. So I propose to revert the change from bug 564335.

buginformation.summary: WizardProjectsImportPage does not correctly set fields when switching to import archieve
buginformation.description:
When switching the WizardProjectsImportPage to the import from archive mode the first two checkboxes in the Options area (i.e., Search for nested projects and Copy projects into workspace) are selected and deactivated. However the functionality behind these two checkboxes is not activated correctly. Therefore when importing a project from the archive where in the archive is a project with the same name as you already have in workspace the content of the two projects gets merged. However if you first manually turn on the options in the select root directory mode and then switch to import from archive mode the correct checks are performed and any projects in the archive with the same name as a project are greyed out and marked as they can not be imported.
I would expect the latter behavior on both cases.
I looked into the code and when activating the import from archive mode only the check boxes are selected but not the according fields of the import wizard set. I debugged that and it seems that the selection listener for the two checkboxes is not called when invoking setSelection on the checbox.
For me the solution would be to in addition set the fields to have the correct behavior. Will submit a page right afterwards.

buginformation.summary: [Mac] Views like Quick search, Git Pull and History view is not aligned at top and left
buginformation.description:
Git Version : Eclipse EGit Git integration for Eclipse 6.0.0.202111291000-r org.eclipse.egit Eclipse Version - 4.23.0.v20211222-1800
The Tables are not aligned in the top and left for Git History and Git pull view.
Attaching the image for History view.
Same issue with Egit 5.12 version and Eclipse Version - 4.23.0.v20211222-1800

buginformation.summary: [Databinding] Add ComputedList/Set.create
buginformation.description:
The ComputedValue class has gotten a utility method to create a ComputedValue from a lambda:
ComputedValue.create(() -) ...)
We should add corresponding methods to ComputedList and ComputedSet.

buginformation.summary: Widget Factory for browser
buginformation.description: I think we should also add a factory for the SWT Browser widgets in JFace similar to the ButtonFactory.

buginformation.summary: Reduce IDE dependencies of org.eclipse.ui.monitoring to make it usable in RCP application
buginformation.description: Would be nice to be able to use the freeze monitor also in e4 RCP applications. for this we should try to reduce / remove the dependencies to the IDE / e3 API.

buginformation.summary: MarkerSupportView should allow custom open method for markers.
buginformation.description: Like most of the methods in ExtendedMarkersView the openSelectedMarkers is package private. This means it is not possible to have a custom method of opening the editor. This could be easily fixed by making that method protected. This would allow any views implementing MarkerSupportView to implement their own method of opening the correct editor.

buginformation.summary: SWTError: No more handles if "Run in background" is disabled
buginformation.description:
Created attachment 287900 Error log after the failure
Test steps: Uncheck Preferences/General/Always run in background Import all projects of a large PDE Git repository using eGit Check Project/Build automatically Checkout another branch a few times using eGit
On each checkout mouse pointer becomes a progress indicator thousands of times (blinks in and out of progress indication). Eventually, SWT handles run out and multiple exceptions are logged (see error.log).
I've captured jstack output for an Eclipse process that failed shortly after (see jstack.txt). In this case, a resource navigation dialog was opened, but I think this happens even without dialogs. I will try to capture more jstacks to find a pattern.
Eclipse IDE for Eclipse Committers (includes Incubating components)
Version: 2022-03 M1 (4.23.0 M1) Build id: 20220113-1818

buginformation.summary: [Navigator] NestedProjectsProblemsModel: Cache removal breaks for IResources without location
buginformation.description:
The
NestedProjectsProblemsModel#removeFromCache(Set(IResource))
implementation currently uses
IResource#getLocation()
to get IPath objects to call
IPath#isPrefixOf(IPath)
to check parent-child relationships.
However, as the Javadoc of getLocation() states, this may return null for resources that do not have a physical local location, e.g. for IResources based on a custom IFileSystem implementation such as the Eclipse Semantic File System (SFS).
As a result, error markers on project level do not disappear, even when the actual errors are resolved.
Instead of getLocation() getFullPath() should be used, which is never null, and cheaper than getLocation().

buginformation.summary: report progress for "System: Decoration Calculation"
buginformation.description: bug 533970 added a progress bar for Decoration Calculation but did not report any progress. to reproduce see https://bugs.eclipse.org/bugs/show_bug.cgi?id=533970#c3

buginformation.summary: [Accessibility] Model Spy Form tab has text labels which JAWS doesn't read
buginformation.description:
Created attachment 288008 JAWS recording, the issue can be shown around at 50 secs
On Form tab of Model Spy, Application default tab has text label with check box, "To Be Rendered" and "Visible". JAWS doesn't read these labels.

buginformation.summary: Stable Sort Order in Progress View
buginformation.description:
If there are frequently Jobs appearing in the Progress View they might get inserted/removed above a long runnning Job that i want to cancel. That makes it sometimes impossible to hit the right Job. Happens especially if i have enabled to show sleeping/system Jobs which frequently pop up.
I suggest to have a visual stable Sort order, i.e. Jobs that are once shown keep shown at the same place or above but are never moved down. i.e. new Jobs should never be visually added above an already shown job. "visually" because they might only have been filtered away.

buginformation.summary: Large file association not correctly removed if disabled beforehand
buginformation.description:
Steps to reproduce below.
In a clean workspace, from the preference page:
1. Define a large file association for .java files, an editor prompt for files of size above 8 bytes. 1.1. Open a .java file to see that the preference applies. 2. Apply. 3. Disable the .java preference. 3.1. Open a .java file, see that the preference is disabled. 4. Apply. 5. Remove the .java preference. 5.1. Observe that the preference still exists for .java files, although it was removed.

buginformation.summary: Remove BookmarkNavigator and related API
buginformation.description: Continuation of bug 550439 .

buginformation.summary: Eclipse hangs when preferences dialog closed after selecting Appearance page
buginformation.description:
Happens with Eclipse 4.20 and also with latest I-build - I20220216-1800
Steps to reproduce: 1. Open Eclipse in dark theme 2. Open a Java class in the Editor 3. Open Preferences ) General ) Appearance 4. Close or cancel the Preference dialog
Eclipse just hangs. Doesn't happen if I close all the files in the editor and try the same steps. Also, was not able to reproduce on Light theme.
It looks like Eclipse is trying to set a theme, though nothing was changed and the the dialog was cancelled.

buginformation.summary: Toggle visibility of window toolbars/main toolbar/status bar have strange interferences
buginformation.description:
I'm facing some strange interferences and anomalies while using the options above.
(Background: I was wondering how to achieve a kind of "zen mode" in Eclipse and came up with combining "full screen/maximize editor/hide toolbars/hide status bar" might do the trick ...)
Things I notices:
* "Toggle visibility of status bar" does hide the status bar, but not show it again when invoked a second time. It does show again if I e.g. toggle the main toolbar or maximize the window. --) looks like a refresh of the window is missing here
* "Toggle visibility of the window toolbars" does also hide the main toolbar, yet a subsequent "toggle main toolbar visibility" does not bring the main toolbar back to visibility, but seems to confirm the state "it should really be hidden". Calling "toggle main toolbar visibility" again then makes the main toolbar visible again.
* "Toggle visibility of the window toolbar" has a very annoying side effect: it blocks other views to show up when invoked by keyboard. E.g. if I press Ctrl-Shift-G in a Java Editor to find references in the workspace I would expect the view with the found references to show up. Yet nothing happens! I have to enable the visibility of the window toolbar again, before I can actually use this keyboard shortcut again. (While this might ensure my intended "Zen mode" to actually be only focused on my current code, it's more focus than I was looking for ;-)...)
I was not sure if I should create one or several issues for this, as these things seem to be somewhat strangely interrelated.

buginformation.summary: [MinMaxAddon] NPE on maximize with sub Area
buginformation.description:
Created attachment 288088 E4 Model Setup
When Double Clicking on the Header of an Part that is nested inside a Composite Part with an own Area an NPE is thrown.
See the Attached Screenshot for the E4 Part Setup.
Form my analysis it looks like that the MinMaxAddon looks for the element to maximize, in most cases the found element is the Editorss Area, from this then the SharedRef is used without a Null check.
But the Element that is found in my use case is the nested Area inside the Composite Part. Which does not have a SharedRef because it is directly attached below the Composite Part.
My desired behavior would be that the Editorss Area is found and maximized. This already works for the Hot Key Ctrl+M

buginformation.summary: remove fake job "The user is waiting for background work to complete" while waiting
buginformation.description:
When the "User Operation is waiting" Dialog pops up to allow canceling of Jobs then there is a Job called "The user is waiting for background work to complete".
It's purely fake and does not add any value. The text is even sometimes wrong. (see example screenshot: Should be the same as the Info item - 'is waiting for "Building"'). Using the cancel button on that entry is the same as pressing the dedicated "Cancel user Operation" Button. It's confusing to have two ways to cancel the waiting job. I prefer to only have the cancel button and the correct text is in the headline anyway.
The only value it might ever had that in principle it could had displayed a progressbar how many jobs we are waiting for. But that does not work either, as it only would try to count down while additional jobs can and do still start as long as they do not conflicting.

buginformation.summary: ViewLabelProvider - java.lang.Error: SWT Resource was not properly disposed
buginformation.description:
I see the following stacktrace if I use the show view dialog in my runtime Eclipse.
!ENTRY org.eclipse.ui.ide 4 4 2022-02-22 07:45:02.312 !MESSAGE Not properly disposed SWT resource !STACK 0 java.lang.Error: SWT Resource was not properly disposed at org.eclipse.swt.graphics.Resource.initNonDisposeTracking(Resource.java:172) at org.eclipse.swt.graphics.Resource.(init)(Resource.java:120) at org.eclipse.swt.graphics.Image.(init)(Image.java:683) at org.eclipse.jface.resource.URLImageDescriptor.createImage(URLImageDescriptor.java:275) at org.eclipse.jface.resource.DeferredImageDescriptor.createImage(DeferredImageDescriptor.java:80) at org.eclipse.jface.resource.ImageDescriptor.createImage(ImageDescriptor.java:290) at org.eclipse.jface.resource.ImageDescriptor.createImage(ImageDescriptor.java:268) at org.eclipse.ui.internal.dialogs.ViewLabelProvider.getImage(ViewLabelProvider.java:102) at org.eclipse.jface.viewers.ColumnLabelProvider.update(ColumnLabelProvider.java:41) at org.eclipse.jface.viewers.ViewerColumn.refresh(ViewerColumn.java:144) at org.eclipse.jface.viewers.AbstractTreeViewer.doUpdateItem(AbstractTreeViewer.java:957) at org.eclipse.jface.viewers.AbstractTreeViewer$UpdateItemSafeRunnable.run(AbstractTreeViewer.java:126) at org.eclipse.core.runtime.SafeRunner.run(SafeRunner.java:45) at org.eclipse.jface.util.SafeRunnable.run(SafeRunnable.java:174) at org.eclipse.jface.viewers.AbstractTreeViewer.doUpdateItem(AbstractTreeViewer.java:1035) at org.eclipse.jface.viewers.StructuredViewer$UpdateItemSafeRunnable.run(StructuredViewer.java:427) at org.eclipse.core.runtime.SafeRunner.run(SafeRunner.java:45) at org.eclipse.jface.util.SafeRunnable.run(SafeRunnable.java:174) at org.eclipse.jface.viewers.StructuredViewer.updateItem(StructuredViewer.java:2109) at org.eclipse.jface.viewers.AbstractTreeViewer.createTreeItem(AbstractTreeViewer.java:851) at org.eclipse.jface.viewers.AbstractTreeViewer.createChildren(AbstractTreeViewer.java:829) at org.eclipse.jface.viewers.TreeViewer.createChildren(TreeViewer.java:611) at org.eclipse.jface.viewers.AbstractTreeViewer.createChildren(AbstractTreeViewer.java:777) at org.eclipse.jface.viewers.AbstractTreeViewer.internalInitializeTree(AbstractTreeViewer.java:1597) at org.eclipse.jface.viewers.TreeViewer.internalInitializeTree(TreeViewer.java:787) at org.eclipse.jface.viewers.AbstractTreeViewer.lambda$1(AbstractTreeViewer.java:1582) at org.eclipse.jface.viewers.StructuredViewer.preservingSelection(StructuredViewer.java:1398) at org.eclipse.jface.viewers.TreeViewer.preservingSelection(TreeViewer.java:365) at org.eclipse.jface.viewers.StructuredViewer.preservingSelection(StructuredViewer.java:1359) at org.eclipse.jface.viewers.AbstractTreeViewer.inputChanged(AbstractTreeViewer.java:1576) at org.eclipse.ui.dialogs.FilteredTree$NotifyingTreeViewer.inputChanged(FilteredTree.java:966) at org.eclipse.jface.viewers.ContentViewer.setInput(ContentViewer.java:282) at org.eclipse.jface.viewers.StructuredViewer.setInput(StructuredViewer.java:1632) at org.eclipse.ui.internal.dialogs.ShowViewDialog.createFilteredTreeViewer(ShowViewDialog.java:226) at org.eclipse.ui.internal.dialogs.ShowViewDialog.createDialogArea(ShowViewDialog.java:164) at org.eclipse.jface.dialogs.Dialog.createContents(Dialog.java:767) at org.eclipse.jface.window.Window.create(Window.java:431) at org.eclipse.jface.dialogs.Dialog.create(Dialog.java:1094) at org.eclipse.jface.window.Window.open(Window.java:788) at org.eclipse.ui.handlers.ShowViewHandler.openOther(ShowViewHandler.java:106) at org.eclipse.ui.handlers.ShowViewHandler.execute(ShowViewHandler.java:81) at org.eclipse.ui.internal.handlers.HandlerProxy.execute(HandlerProxy.java:283) at org.eclipse.ui.internal.handlers.E4HandlerProxy.execute(E4HandlerProxy.java:97) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:77) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:43) at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:568) at org.eclipse.e4.core.internal.di.MethodRequestor.execute(MethodRequestor.java:58) at org.eclipse.e4.core.internal.di.InjectorImpl.invokeUsingClass(InjectorImpl.java:317) at org.eclipse.e4.core.internal.di.InjectorImpl.invoke(InjectorImpl.java:251) at org.eclipse.e4.core.contexts.ContextInjectionFactory.invoke(ContextInjectionFactory.java:173) at org.eclipse.core.commands.ParameterizedCommand.executeWithChecks(ParameterizedCommand.java:487) at org.eclipse.e4.core.commands.internal.HandlerServiceImpl.executeHandler(HandlerServiceImpl.java:213) at org.eclipse.ui.internal.handlers.LegacyHandlerService.executeCommand(LegacyHandlerService.java:389) at org.eclipse.ui.internal.ShowViewMenu$1.run(ShowViewMenu.java:132) at org.eclipse.jface.action.Action.runWithEvent(Action.java:474) at org.eclipse.jface.action.ActionContributionItem.handleWidgetSelection(ActionContributionItem.java:580) at org.eclipse.jface.action.ActionContributionItem.lambda$4(ActionContributionItem.java:414) at org.eclipse.swt.widgets.EventTable.sendEvent(EventTable.java:89) at org.eclipse.swt.widgets.Display.sendEvent(Display.java:4243) at org.eclipse.swt.widgets.Widget.sendEvent(Widget.java:1060) at org.eclipse.swt.widgets.Display.runDeferredEvents(Display.java:4060) at org.eclipse.swt.widgets.Display.readAndDispatch(Display.java:3632) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.internal.workbench.swt.PartRenderingEngine$5.run(PartRenderingEngine.java:1154) at org.eclipse.equinox.launcher.Main.main(Main.java:1440)

buginformation.summary: NPE in MenuManagerHideProcessor.java:105
buginformation.description:
i do not know how it happend or how to reproduce, but it was in my logfile.
According to the stacktrace it happend during closing eclipse, where a null value for currentMenuElement.getObject() seems plausible:
currentMenuElement.getTransientData().get(MenuManagerShowProcessor.DYNAMIC_ELEMENT_STORAGE_KEY); may return null
java.lang.NullPointerException at org.eclipse.e4.core.internal.di.InjectorImpl.invoke(InjectorImpl.java:251) at buginformation.summary: [JFace] Clicking on NotificationPopup should reveal parent window
Widget is disposed exception from SWTPartRenderer during unit tests for disposed toolbar entries
buginformation.description:
During unit test exececution we see Widget is disposed excpetions from SWTPartRenderer.
I guess we should also check here if the widget is disposed before setting a CSS class.
!ENTRY org.eclipse.e4.ui.workbench 4 0 2022-03-02 09:47:41.961 !MESSAGE Exception occurred while rendering: ch.belimo.bd.tools.pagedesigner.toolbar=org.eclipse.e4.ui.model.application.ui.menu.impl.ToolBarImpl@7057dbda (tags: [Draggable], contributorURI: null) (widget: ImageBasedFrame {}, renderer: org.eclipse.e4.ui.workbench.renderers.swt.ToolBarManagerRenderer@71d0b8a4, toBeRendered: true, onTop: false, visible: false, containerData: null, accessibilityPhrase: null) !STACK 0 org.eclipse.swt.SWTException: Widget is disposed at org.eclipse.swt.SWT.error(SWT.java:4907) at org.eclipse.swt.SWT.error(SWT.java:4822) at org.eclipse.swt.SWT.error(SWT.java:4793) at org.eclipse.swt.widgets.Widget.error(Widget.java:561) at org.eclipse.swt.widgets.Widget.checkWidget(Widget.java:476) at org.eclipse.swt.widgets.Widget.setData(Widget.java:1745) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.css.swt.dom.WidgetElement.setCSSClass(WidgetElement.java:70) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.internal.workbench.swt.PartRenderingEngine$7.setClassnameAndId(PartRend at org.eclipse.e4.ui.workbench.renderers.swt.SWTPartRenderer.setCSSInfo(SWTPartRenderer.java:114) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.workbench.renderers.swt.ToolBarManagerRenderer.createWidget(ToolBarManagerRenderer.java:425) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.internal.workbench.swt.PartRenderingEngine.createWidget(PartRenderingEngine.java:994) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.internal.workbench.swt.PartRenderingEngine.safeCreateGui(PartRenderingEngine.java:658) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.internal.workbench.swt.PartRenderingEngine.safeCreateGui(PartRenderingEngine.java:762) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.internal.workbench.swt.PartRenderingEngine$2.run(PartRenderingEngine.java:727) at org.eclipse.core.runtime.SafeRunner.run(SafeRunner.java:45) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.internal.workbench.swt.PartRenderingEngine.createGui(PartRenderingEngine.java:711) at org.eclipse.e4.ui.internal.workbench.swt.PartRenderingEngine.subscribeChildrenHandler(PartRenderingEngine.java:311) at jdk.internal.reflect.GeneratedMethodAccessor9.invoke(Unknown Source) at java.base/jdk.int

buginformation.summary: Large file association editor prompt appears even when disabled
buginformation.description:
Using 2022-03 RC1 Eclipse IDE for Java Developers it seems that the check box
Default limit [ ] For not listed types, prompt for editor if the size exceeds
is ignored and it always checks the listed size.
E.g. set enabled and size to 8B, prompt appears for a small Java file. set disabled, leave size to 8B, prompt appears for a small Java file. set enabled and size to 80000B, prompt does not appear for a small Java file. prompt does appear for a large file. set disabled, leave size to 80000B, prompt does not appear for a small Java file. prompt does appear for a large file.
Also, I think the large file association options might be inconvenient for Eclipse Memory Analyzer, bug 579110.
Currently MAT uses content types and associated editors, and parsers for the different dump types are pluggable.
Having a plug-in customization with a list of values doesn't seem extensible org.eclipse.ui.workbench/largeFileLimits_types=java,xml,txt
How would each parser plugin independently say it correctly handles large files of the type it processes?
Also the matching on file extensions seems to just match the last part of the extension, and not the content type, or a double extension.
E.g. the MAT HPROF parser handles *.hprof *.hprof.gz files. It seems that I would have to match all *.gz files to get the HPROF parser to handle *.hprof.gz files, which doesn't seem right.

buginformation.summary: Large file editor selection dialog cannot be cancelled after remembering an editor selection
buginformation.description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. Enable large file associations default limit, set it to 1 byte. 2. Open some file, select some external editor (e.g. Emacs on Linux) and remember selection. 3. In the Large File Associations preference page, delete the newly added preference and apply. 4. Open the file again, cancel the prompt dialog. 5. Observe that the previously chosen editor is still chosen.
This occurs for any file type, until an IDE restart.

buginformation.summary: [Multiple carets] Commands for multi selection not available in pom.xml editor
buginformation.description:
When opening a pom.xml file in the source code view of the pom-editor the multi-selection commands are not accessible/actionable. This is true for - "To Multi-selection" - The new multi selection commands currently under development like "Add next match to multi selection"
I guess the pom-editor is part of m2e, but I'd need a little help where to look further. Doesn't the pom-editor use StyledText and thus inherit it's capabilities? I couldn't easily orient in the multitude of m2e-packages -- can anyone help me out where to start?

buginformation.summary: Welcome screen should not hide toolbar
buginformation.description: Opening the welcome screen hides the toolbar which I as a user find inconsistent. As the welcome screen is the only view which does this, I suggest to remove this due to a consistent behavior.

buginformation.summary: Remove dead logic from CSSPropertyBackgroundSWTHandler / AbstractCSSPropertyBackgroundHandler
buginformation.description: I'm currently looking into Bug 577463 and Bug 577493. CSSPropertyBackgroundSWTHandler / AbstractCSSPropertyBackgroundHandler contains some dummy / generated code without any logic which makes it harder to follow the execution path. This code is ancient and unnecessary so it should be removed.

buginformation.summary: MarkerSupportView: It should be possible to determine if a filter has been set on the MarkerSupportView through the FiltersConfigurationDialog.
buginformation.description:
For our use case we require to know if a filter has been set on the MarkerSupportView through the FiltersConfigurationDialog.
From searching the code, I see that enabledFilters in MarkerContentGenerator is a collection of all the filters that have been enabled through the FiltersConfigurationDialog.
The issue is that both MarkerContentGenerator and enabledFilters are not accessible through the Eclipse API and so it is not possible for us to determine if there is a filter set on the MarkerSupportView.

buginformation.summary: IES423:(ICT) Missing space between two strings
buginformation.description:
Created attachment 288336 missing_space_CSY
Build: I20220323-1001
Problem: CSY Testers requested to have spaces between two strings.
Steps to reproduce: Double click in desktop to open Eclipse client. Click Launch with default workspace Click Windows -) Preference in the top menu Click 'Model Editor' in the left
Please see attachment for details
